items.headline: US Stock Futures Subdued Ahead of Key Inflation Data, Middle East Tensions Persist
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are largely muted this morning as investors await the crucial March Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, scheduled for release at 8:30 a.m. ET. The CPI is anticipated to show a 3.3% annual increase, a figure that could further influence the Federal Reserve's stance on interest rate easing, with money market participants currently not pricing in any policy reductions for 2026. Despite today's subdued pre-market activity, Wall Street's main indexes closed higher on Thursday, buoyed by hopes for de-escalation in the Middle East following Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's comments on seeking direct talks with Beirut. The S&P 500 is positioned for its largest weekly gain since November, and the Dow for its sharpest weekly increase since June. Overall market sentiment remains cautious, however, as a two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran shows signs of strain ahead of weekend talks. US stocks are currently trading near a 5-week high, cautiously holding recent gains amid geopolitical developments.

sections.title: Major Indices (Pre-market as of 04:45 a.m. ET)

items.headline: Invivyd ($IVVD) Surges on Biotech Progress
items.summary: Invivyd (IVVD) saw a significant pre-market gain of +31.85%, likely driven by positive clinical or regulatory developments in infectious disease treatments.

items.headline: Eos Energy Enterprises ($EOSE) Boosted by Energy Transition Demand
items.summary: Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) rose +29.63% pre-market, experiencing a high volume surge attributed to increasing demand for long-duration battery and energy storage solutions.

items.headline: Captivision ($CAPT) Plummets Amid Volatility
items.summary: Captivision (CAPT) experienced a substantial drop of approximately -75% pre-market. This extreme volatility is common in speculative stocks and could be due to profit-taking, dilution, or negative news.

items.headline: Actelis Networks ($ASNS) Declines Sharply
items.summary: Actelis Networks (ASNS), a tech and networking company, fell around -73% in pre-market trading. Sharp declines in low-priced stocks can occur due to low volume or adverse events.

items.headline: March CPI Release Expected Today, Inflation Concerns Remain
items.summary: The U.S. will release its March Consumer Price Index (CPI) today at 8:30 a.m. ET. Economists anticipate an annual CPI increase of 3.3%, which, if higher than expected, could make it challenging for the Federal Reserve to implement interest rate cuts in the near term. Energy prices were a significant contributor to inflation in March, rising 12.5% year over year.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Kicks Off Next Week with Major Banks
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season officially begins next week. Major U.S. banks are set to report, with Goldman Sachs on Monday, followed by JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo on Tuesday. Bank of America and Morgan Stanley will report on Wednesday. The S&P 500 Financials Index is projected to show robust Q1 earnings growth of approximately 16%, surpassing the overall index average of 12.5%.

items.headline: ExxonMobil Q1 Earnings to Benefit from Elevated Oil Prices
items.summary: ExxonMobil ($XOM) anticipates that higher oil and gas prices will bolster its first-quarter upstream earnings by as much as $2.9 billion. This surge in commodity prices is expected to offset a 6% reduction in global oil-equivalent production for the quarter, largely due to disruptions at assets in the United Arab Emirates and Qatar. Oil prices are currently trading around the high-$90s per barrel amidst geopolitical supply concerns.

items.headline: Middle East Geopolitical Developments Continue to Influence Markets
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly concerns surrounding the Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz, remain a significant factor impacting market sentiment. The fragile two-week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is under scrutiny as talks are scheduled for this weekend.
